rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA JOAN FARMER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel San Jose Water Company To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel San Jose Water Company to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents is continued to December 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 per agreement of the parties. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HOFF et al VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Kelly Moore Paint Company, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s joinder in Kelly Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens conveniens is off calendar given the ruling on the underlying motion. This hearing on the underlying motion will be at 9 am, not 9:30am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant The Boeing Company; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of the person most qualified of Boeing Co. is continued to December 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 to give the parties time to meet and confer in an effort to reach agreement on the scope of the deposition(s) and documents to be produced that is focused on OEM or Boeing-supplied replacement asbestos-containing parts of Boeing airplanes that it is reasonably possible that Mr. Gauger was exposed to during his career as a mechanic. As it is now written, the deposition notice is overbroad and imposes undue burden on Boeing. If any issues remain at that time, by 4:30pm on December 1, 2010 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain unresolved and then appear at the hearing at 9am on December 2. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant The Boeing Companys Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special And Form Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admissions; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, November 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel further responses from Boeing Co. is denied. Plaintiffs failed to engage in a good faith meet and confer effort priot to filing the motion. Sanctions of $1,000 are awarded in favor of defendant. The hearing on this motion will be at 8:30 a.m., not 9:00 a.m. = (220/HEK)
